channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Hello Neighbor 3 Pre-Alpha is Available Now
item.content:encoded:
tinyBuild has announced that Hello Neighbor 3’s pre-alpha begins on Steam today; here’s what’s included.

The developers released Prototype 3 for the horror title back in November 2025, and the game’s development seems to have progressed well since then, with the developers entering pre-alpha in less than 6 months. The latest version features a new mission in Raven Brooks, changes to the objective system, characters, and more.

Hello Neighbor 3 Pre-Alpha Introduces a New Mission, Overhauled Objective System, Improved Characters, and More

The Hello Neighbor 3 pre-alpha features a new corporate mission in Raven Brooks, where players are looking for a missing employee who holds the location of an important report. However, players end up unearthing a bigger mystery related to the constant disappearances in Raven Brooks.

The objective system has been overhauled, where players are guided through the narrative in a way that Hello Neighbor series’ “free roam spirit is retained. The loop-based progression will free players from speed running. The loops also end seamlessly, restarting with objectives and knowledge learned in the previous run.

Characters have been updated, too, where their behavior is now more predictable, and sneaking feels more fun. Players will also find new locations, items, and visual upgrades in Raven Brooks in the pre-alpha.

The developers have also emphasized the game being the best entry point in the series, with Nikita Kolesnikov, the franchise creator, stating, “We’re building Hello Neighbor 3 around the idea that there is no wrong way to play.”

Everything New in Hello Neighbor 3 Pre-Alpha

Check out everything that’s changed since Prototype 3 to Hello Neighbor 3’s Pre-Alpha below:

  • Progressing through the game is now much easier. The new objective system helps you to follow the narrative while keeping the free-roam nature of the game.
  • No need to speedrun to beat the game. Now you can progress gradually across multiple “loops”. The end of the “loop” is not a game over but rather a seamless restart with your current objectives carrying over.
  • Improved characters’ behavior. Actions are more deliberate and predictable. Also, sneaking works better!
  • The Guest’s behavior and gameplay are now more interesting 
  • Map updated with new locations and improved existing ones
  • Many objects and characters now have high-quality visuals
  • New items added and existing ones reworked
  • Fall damage adds complexity and planning to your exploring (we promise)
  • Canon story, including the Protagonist!

What are your thoughts on Hello Neighbor 3 pre-alpha going live on Steam? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Division starting off as a World of Warcraft-style MMO. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Invincible VS Devs Address How They’ll Handle Rage Quits
item.content:encoded:
The developers of Invincible VS have revealed how they are dealing with rage quitting in the soon-to-be-released fighting game. The game recently wrapped up an open beta, and although it was so well-received that it was extended, they also received feedback on the earliest matches and what they called “a storm of Rage Quits.”

The latest notes following the beta discuss the feedback they received from players and outline what they will do going forward to address the amount of rage-quitting.

Fixes to Rage Quitting in Invincible VS

For starters, in order to address future rage-quitting issues, disconnecting during a ranked match will cost players League Points. Furthermore, they’re looking at ways to keep those who frequently quit matches out of the ranked queue. They also say they are working with Quarter Up to implement a cooldown penalty, targeting the first major post-launch patch.

“If you’re reading this and were guilty of a rage quit or two during the beta…we get it. Ranked was the system we most needed to stress test, but we also know that meant players who wanted to explore at their own pace (or who are new to fighting games entirely) didn’t have a good way to do that,” they said.

Additional fixes include fewer ghost players in matchmaking queues. Also, a majority of issues that caused the abated to crash have been fixed. Moving forward, they’re excited for further announcements regarding roster inclusions leading up to the release on April 30.

The developers say Invincible VS is for everyone, and they hope the wide variety of game modes will let all players try it out and develop their skills along the way.

Are you excited for this game’s release? Did you play the Invincible VS beta? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: The Division Started Off as a World of Warcraft-Style MMO
item.content:encoded:
During a new interview, the developers of the original Division discussed its origins as an MMO like World of Warcraft.

The Division franchise has had a lot of promising updates in 2026, with a Definitive Edition for the first game shadowdropped in February and a roadmap for The Division 2 featuring crossplay, DLC, and a fresh extraction experience that arrived last month. However, the franchise was not always the third-person looter-shooter that we know and love today.

Spotted by Kotaku, The Division’s World of Warcraft-style MMO origins were mentioned during a new video posted by Massive Entertainment featuring the devs of the original game. The developers first discussed their main combat loop for both games, OPE, which stands for Observe, Plan, and Execute. However, the developers took a while to reach that because when they started working on The Division, “it was still an MMO like World of Warcraft.

At this point, a short clip started playing, showcasing early development footage of the MMO version of The Division. It featured the player taking on multiple enemies while moving around with a dog companion that could attack them. The visual style was also very different, opting for a more vibrant setting rather than the dark and gritty version we received in the final product.

The developers also explained that at that point, the game didn’t include the skill component or traditional shooter aspects. They also mentioned that the game had hotbars during its early stages of development. Interested readers can check out the full interview below:

In other news, The Division 2 has skyrocketed in terms of player count since Ubisoft dropped the new roadmap for 2026. Do you like what The Division franchise is today, or should the developers have stuck to its MMO origins? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Gears of War’s Netflix adaptation receiving a promising update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Gears of War Netflix Adaptation Receives Promising Update
item.content:encoded:
The director of Netflix’s Gears of War movie has stated that the draft is in great shape and thinks it’s going to happen in a new update.

For those unaware, a report last year stated that Fall Guy director David Leitch was in negotiations with Netflix to direct the upcoming Gears of War movie. It seems things have progressed much further since then, with the director almost certain that the movie will come to fruition.

“It’s All Lining Up That That Movie Will Happen,” Says Gears of War Movie Director in Latest Update

During a new interview with Collider at CinemaCon, David Leitch provided fans with a brand-new update on the Gears of War movie from Netflix. Leitch stated that he thinks it’s “going to happen”. The director added that they have a draft that is in “great shape”, the studio is more determined, and Netflix is “100% behind it it” Moreover, Coalition is “fired up” as well, and with Gears of War E-Day releasing this year, “it’s all lining up that that movie will happen.” Check out the full quote below:

“Gears of War, I think, is going to happen. We have a great draft that’s in progress that’s in really great shape. The studio’s more determined than ever to make it. Netflix is 100% behind it. The Coalition is fired up, as well. They have their game releasing this year, as well, and so it’s all lining up that that movie will happen.”

The report from last year stated that it would be a big-budget adaptation, with the movie remaining faithful to the Gears of War source material. Netflix acquired the rights to the film in 2022 and brought on Jon Spaihts, writer of Dune 1 & 2, Doctor Strange, and more, as one of the scriptwriters.

In other news, Gears of War E-Day gave the legendary dev, Cliff Bleszinski, goosebumps. Additionally, the runtime of the upcoming Gears of War: E-Day presentation has been revealed by the Xbox CCO. What are your thoughts on the new update for Netflix’s Gears of War movie adaptation? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about The Witcher 3’s Zoltan voice actor passing away. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
